name: Leonese
notes: Modern Leonese (Llionés), spoken in the provinces of León, Zamora, and Salamanca (Spain). Inherits all grapheme→phoneme correspondences from ast-x-leon, which is grounded in Morala & Egido (2009) and the Propuesta de Norma Ortográfica. Key values: ⟨x⟩=/ʃ/, ⟨ll⟩=/ʎ/, ⟨ch⟩=/tʃ/ (PL-/CL-/FL-), ⟨y⟩=/ʝ/ (-LJ- general), ⟨ñ⟩=/ɲ/. Western varieties retain ei/ou diphthongs and -u final (see ast-x-occidental). Related to Mirandese (Portugal), which descends from the same Leonese branch.

sources.author: Morala Rodríguez, J.R. & Egido Fernández, M.C.
sources.title: A vueltas con una norma para el leonés
sources.publisher: Instituto Castellano y Leonés de la Lengua, Colección Beltenebros 23
sources.notes: ISBN: 978-84-936383-8-2. Descriptive survey confirming grapheme values from Leonese corpora.

sources.author: Anonymous
sources.title: Propuesta de Norma Ortográfica para el Leonés
sources.notes: Prescriptive proposal, western Leonese base. Year is approximate (undated document).
